creator: mwisnicki+freebsd@gmail.com
text:
On Tue, Sep 7, 2010 at 22:06, Marcin Wisnicki
<mwisnicki+freebsd@gmail.com> wrote:
> On Tue, Sep 7, 2010 at 21:38, =C2=A0<dinoex@freebsd.org> wrote:
>> Synopsis: print/cups-base: SSL support missing
>>
>> cups does not use the opensssl comatibility layer when buils with gnutls=
.
>
> You are right, I was mislead by a outdated information from debian packag=
e.
>
>>
>> OpenSSL is default Off, beacuse the GPL does not allow redistribution wi=
th OpenSSL linked in.
>>
>
> CUPS license has explicit exception for linking with OpenSSL. [1]
> And even if it didn't have one, OpenSSL in FreeBSD is a system
> component thus excepted under section 3 of GPL-2.
>
> =C2=A0[1]. http://www.cups.org/documentation.php/license.html
>
>>
>> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=3D150310
>>
>

Oh, and by the way we should probably warn not to depend on encryption
in cups-client since cups library does not verify certificates.
It's documented upstream
http://www.cups.org/documentation.php/doc-1.4/security.html and in
http://www.cups.org/str.php?L3093.
Using SSL on server is safe though (useful for administration and
using MS-Windows IPP clients).
